theoremName: abstract active projection convergence consequence theorem
statement: The compact-source spectral projection theorem implies norm convergence of active Riesz projections and, by subtraction from the identity, norm convergence of inactive projections.

activeRieszProjectionConvergenceStatus.label: active Riesz projection convergence
activeRieszProjectionConvergenceStatus.reason: Imported from the compact-source spectral projection theorem: the isolated active source cluster has stable Riesz projections and active/inactive splitting.

inactiveProjectionConvergenceStatus.label: inactive projection convergence
inactiveProjectionConvergenceStatus.reason: If P_active,N -> P_active in norm, then I-P_active,N -> I-P_active in norm with the same norm error.

proof: Import stability of the active/inactive source splitting.
Subtract active projections from the identity to get inactive projection convergence.
Expose only the projection-convergence consequence needed by the min-max tail passage.
